# Transcrição: Data hack - copying views across accounts

- **Fonte:** Data hack - copying views across accounts
- **Duração:** 02:10
- **Idioma:** en

## [00:00 - 02:10]

Hey, this is a quick video of a really cool, really simple, but really useful hack to get your metrics working across accounts. So there's an account here which, so you can see these are the default columns. I've got a view set up already and what I will do now is actually take that view that I've got and transfer that into an account which I don't have a view set up. So I'm going to click into that now and just show you how simple it is. What Facebook does, I don't know whether this was by design or whether it's just consequential, it carries over those columns. So I've just switched into a different account and those columns have carried over. And what this means is I can now save that and quite quickly I've got my columns already set up and I can switch this across many accounts quite quickly. The only thing is the save button doesn't appear until you make an edit on your column. So you have to just quickly go in, take something out, add something in, whatever is easier, let's just add clicks all which I generally don't follow. I do actually need to add one in so I'm just going to find something I just want to add to the end. Let's see what is a good metric. Or maybe not so good metric. I'll just add a video play. So let's just shove that in at the end and now there's the save option and I'm going to call it main KPI. So the way I like to set columns up is if you've seen the other videos, I like to spread them across into one view. Other people like different views for analyzing different parts. I've got my video engagement metrics here, I've got my messenger opens and I've got my key metrics for my websites as well. So that's pretty much it. So now I can save that and then my metrics are here as I want them to appear and it's just as simple as just then if I want to transfer that to other accounts, I can then open the new account up and then save the column again.
